_meta.notes: Generic location for free-text reincarnations. A village abandoned recently — embers still warm, doors open mid-flight. Five rooms, one path out. Designed to be form-agnostic: a slime can ooze through it, a book can be carried through it, a humanoid can walk through it.

ambientPool: Wind moves through houses with no one in them and finds nothing to push.
A bell rope moves once, slowly, then stops.
Somewhere up the slope, a goat is alive and uncertain whose it is now.
The smell of bread is here, half a day stale, in a kitchen with no people.
The sky is the colour of old paper.

rooms.summary: A round of packed earth at the centre of the village. A well, a notice-board, the remains of a market. Whatever happened to the people happened in a hurry — there are baskets on the ground.
ambientPool: The well rope is taut and damp; someone was drawing water when they left.
The notice-board has three notices, all corners curling. None of them mention what came.
A child's wooden toy is on its side near the basin.

exits.narrative: North, where a forge still glows faintly.
East, toward a small stone chapel with the door ajar.
South, into the half-light of an empty tavern.
West, toward the road out — and what is at the edge of it.

rooms.summary: The smith left mid-strike. A hammer is balanced on the anvil. The forge is still warm enough to taste.
ambientPool: The bellows are still inflated, half their air not yet given up.
Tools are arranged in the order of a person who has done this every day for thirty years.
On the workbench, a half-shaped horseshoe and a name carved into the wood: Berra.

rooms.summary: Cool inside, in a way the day outside is not. Pews mostly empty; one has a prayer book left open on it. The altar bears something that should not be on an altar.
ambientPool: Light through the thin windows is colder than the day-light should make it.
Whatever is on the altar has not been there long. It is still wet.
A draft moves the open prayer book by one page, then back.

hazards.narrative: What's on the altar is the first useful clue.

rooms.summary: Tables half-cleared. A tap left running, dripping into a tin pail almost full. The taproom is dim and the dim is friendly, even now.
ambientPool: There are coins on three tables. The patrons left without paying — or without thinking.
A lute leans against a stool, one string broken since long before today.
The pail under the tap will overflow in another hour.

exits.narrative: Out the back door, toward the road west.

rooms.summary: The last fence-post of the village leans into the road. The road goes west into hills the colour of wet stone. Something has happened on it — recently. The dust holds the shape.
ambientPool: Hoofprints in the dust, all going the same way, none coming back.
A torn piece of wool is caught on the fence-post.
Past the fence, the road is empty in a way roads do not usually choose to be.
rooms.exitNarrative: You leave the village. Whatever this was, it was not for you to fix. Survival, today, is enough.
